meta.comment:
v0.3 promotes 3 of v0.2's 6 proxy entries to direct labels using SWE-bench experiments (per-submission, per-instance pass/fail across 500 Verified instances). Direct columns added for claude-sonnet-4-5 (4 SWE-bench submissions mapped to the new claude-sonnet-4-5 column, alongside the v0.2 claude-sonnet-4 column which now also picks up 4 more SWE-bench submissions of the August 2025 claude-sonnet-4 family), claude-opus-4-5 (2 submissions, also serves as a closer Anthropic-family proxy for claude-opus-4-7 vs v0.2's gpt-5 proxy), and gemini-3-pro-preview (1 submission). claude-haiku-4-5 stays on the gemini-2.5-flash proxy until a public benchmark with per-instance haiku scores ships. gpt-5.5, gemini-3-flash-preview, gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview stay on existing proxies (no public direct labels yet).

Why two entries with model=claude-sonnet-4-5 (provider=anthropic) but different bench_column values: the trainer's bench_to_deployed map is list-valued and averages records per (prompt, deployed_model) cell. The two bench columns hold disjoint *records* (different SWE-bench submissions and a different OpenRouterBench frontier slice) on a partially overlapping prompt set, so averaging across both columns mixes evidence from the August 2025 sonnet-4 family with the September 2025 sonnet-4.5 release for the same deployed entry. This is multi-source aggregation, not double-counting: no individual record contributes more than once to any cell, and bench_walker.load_bench keys on (prompt, deployed_model) so each record is incremented once. Adding the second column injects sonnet-4-5-specific signal where direct labels exist while keeping the v0.2 sonnet-4 baseline coverage on the OpenRouterBench prompts where it doesn't. claude-opus-4-7 follows the same pattern with two bench columns (gpt-5 + claude-opus-4-5) that contribute distinct evidence to one deployed entry.

deployed_models.proxy_note: no claude-haiku column in OpenRouterBench or SWE-bench experiments; gemini-2.5-flash stands in as the cheapest small-tier proxy until D3 traffic accumulates
no claude-opus-4-7 column in OpenRouterBench; gpt-5 is a frontier proxy
v0.3-new: closer Anthropic-family proxy from SWE-bench experiments. Anthropic Claude 4.5 line is the closest generational and family match for opus-4-7 of any column we have direct labels on

GPT-5.5 (April 2026) ships after the bench cutoff; use gpt-5's column as the closest generational proxy. gpt-5.5 lists at 2x the price of gpt-5, so the α-blend will favor gpt-5 when bench scores tie — that's intentional given we have no direct evidence gpt-5.5 outperforms gpt-5 on these prompts
Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview supersedes Gemini 3 Pro Preview (deprecated 2026-03-09). Bench cutoff predates the 3.x family; gemini-2.5-pro is the v0.2 proxy
v0.3-new: SWE-bench direct labels for the prior gemini-3-pro-preview release. Closer generational match than gemini-2.5-pro; preview-vs-preview rather than 2.5-vs-3.x

Gemini 3 Flash Preview shipped post-bench; use gemini-2.5-flash's column. The bench scores are conservative — Gemini 3 Flash is reportedly stronger and cheaper, so the α-blend will likely under-pick it until D3 traffic recalibrates
Cheapest Google entry; same column as gemini-3-flash-preview but lower cost rebalances the α-blend toward this tier on cost-sensitive clusters
